title: Introduction to AI Tools
content: AI tools are software programs that use Artificial Intelligence to help people complete tasks faster and more easily. Some AI tools can answer questions, while others create images, improve writing, help with coding, or organize information. AI tools are meant to assist people, not completely replace human thinking, and should be used carefully.

title: ChatGPT
content: ChatGPT is an AI chatbot that can answer questions, explain difficult topics, summarize information, and help generate ideas. Many students, professionals, and businesses use ChatGPT for learning, brainstorming, and problem solving. While useful, ChatGPT may sometimes give incorrect information, so answers should be checked when accuracy is important.

title: Google Gemini
content: Google Gemini is an AI assistant created by Google that helps users answer questions, generate ideas, summarize content, and search for information. It connects closely with Google services and can help users research topics or organize information more efficiently.

title: Claude
content: Claude is an AI assistant designed to help users write, think through problems, and understand information clearly. Many users prefer Claude for long explanations, careful writing, and structured responses. Like other AI systems, users should still verify important facts.

title: GitHub Copilot
content: GitHub Copilot is an AI tool designed to help programmers write code more quickly. It suggests lines of code, explains programming concepts, and helps developers solve coding problems. Beginners can use it to learn, but should avoid depending on it completely without understanding the code.

title: Canva AI
content: Canva AI helps users create presentations, posters, images, and designs more quickly. It can generate ideas, suggest layouts, remove backgrounds, and improve creative projects. Students, businesses, and content creators often use Canva AI to make visual work easier.

title: Using AI Tools Responsibly
content: AI tools work best when users think critically and verify important information. Users should avoid copying answers blindly and instead use AI to support learning, creativity, and productivity. Understanding how to ask clear questions and evaluate answers helps people use AI more effectively.
